label: Poisoning

source.org: American Heart Association / American Red Cross / American Heart Association
source.guideline: 2024 First Aid Guidelines + 2025 Adult Basic Life Support Guidelines

clinicalReviewer.note: Authored by Gabriel Adegboyega. Protocol content sourced from AHA/Red Cross 2024 First Aid Guidelines and AHA 2025 Adult BLS Guidelines DOI:10.1161/CIR.0000000000001281 + 10.1161/CIR.0000000000001369. No independent clinical reviewer has verified this file. A qualified clinician review is required before any public deployment.

contraindications: Do not induce vomiting unless poison control tells you to.
Do not give home remedies unless directed.
disclaimer: VITA provides general first aid guidance based on publicly available guidelines from the American Heart Association, American Red Cross, and International Liaison Committee on Resuscitation. This application does not replace professional medical training or advice. Always call emergency services first. Protocol accuracy depends on the guidelines available at the time of last review.

pois_cpr.headline: Start CPR now.
pois_cpr.rationale: An unresponsive poisoned person who is not breathing normally needs CPR immediately.

pois_route_cpr.headline: Switch to adult CPR now.
pois_route_cpr.instruction: Move into the adult CPR flow immediately.

pois_opioid_check.headline: Any chance this was an opioid?
pois_opioid_check.instruction: Think about slow breathing, blue lips, very small pupils, or extreme unresponsiveness.

pois_naloxone_conscious.headline: Give naloxone if you have it.
pois_naloxone_conscious.instruction: Use a nasal spray dose in one nostril, then keep monitoring closely.
pois_naloxone_conscious.rationale: Naloxone addresses likely opioid poisoning, but close observation must continue because breathing can worsen again.

pois_conscious.headline: Call poison control now.
pois_conscious.instruction: Nigeria: LUTH 01-7745853 or UCH 02-2413701. US: 1-800-222-1222. UK: 111. Describe the substance and follow instructions exactly.
doNot: Do not induce vomiting unless poison control tells you to.
Do not give home remedies unless directed.
pois_conscious.rationale: Poison control gives substance-specific advice that is safer than guessing treatment at the scene.

pois_monitor.headline: Stay with them. Follow all instructions.
pois_monitor.instruction: Monitor breathing and consciousness. If they become unresponsive, start CPR.
pois_monitor.rationale: Poisoned patients can worsen quickly, so ongoing monitoring is part of the response even after poison control is contacted.
